_comment: handoff-fanout config (referenced from $HANDOFF_HOME/config.json). Drop unused keys; all fields are optional with safe defaults.
_autoclose_comment: Tab autoclose (handoff-helper VS Code extension) is OFF by default and is NOT toggled here — config.json is read by the python dump/build, while autoclose runs in the bash launchd watcher (install/auto-continue.sh). To opt in, set env HANDOFF_AUTOCLOSE_ENABLED=1, or touch $HANDOFF_HOME/autoclose.enabled (all projects) / $HANDOFF_HOME/<project>/autoclose.enabled (one project). The watcher then opens vscode://dharmaxis.handoff-helper/autoclose?task_id=..&nonce=..&project=.. — install the extension via install/install.sh (or `cd extension && npm run package && code --install-extension handoff-helper.vsix`).
inject_blocks:
## Project red lines (example)
- never edit src/legacy/ without an ADR
- treasury operations must go through journal_service.post_entry()

baseline_hooks._comment: Each hook runs in the workspace dir; the captured output is added to the handoff baseline block. `regex` is optional — when set, the first match group is stored.
baseline_hooks.name: Alembic

roadmap._comment: Optional file whose phase sections are excerpted into the handoff prompt.
